paper_map.title: High-strength Fe–20Mn–Al–C-based Alloys with Low Density
paper_map.abstract_summary: This study investigated the mechanical properties of Fe–20Mn–(10–14)Al–(0–1.8)C quaternary and Fe–20Mn–(10–14)Al–(0.75–1.8)C–5Cr quinary alloys with low density (<7.0 g/cm³). Hardness, cold-workability, and tensile tests were conducted to clarify compositional and microstructural dependences of specific strength. Results showed that increasing Al and C contents raised hardness and tensile strength due to nano-size κ-carbide precipitation during cooling, and the alloys exhibited higher specific strength than conventional steels with good ductility.
paper_map.research_objective: To clarify the compositional and microstructural dependences of the specific strength in Fe–Mn–Al–C(–Cr) alloys for achieving low density and high strength.
main_process_variables: Cooling method: water quenching or air cooling
Tensile testing at room temperature, strain rate 0.5 mm/min
Hot rolling at 1200°C to 1.5 mm sheets
expected_information_axis: processing (annealing temperature, cooling rate, cold reduction)
microstructure (phase constitution, κ-carbide precipitation, α volume fraction)
property (hardness, yield strength, tensile strength, elongation, specific strength)
mechanism (precipitation hardening, work hardening, phase boundary shift by Cr)
figure_evidence (Fig.1 phase diagram with iso-density lines, Fig.8 specific strength vs elongation comparison)
